content: I want to provide support for the streaming configuration in NuShell, but the problem is that NuShell does not support it. We investigated this previously. I want to create an issue on the NuGet GitHub repository asking for support. Obviously, I would need to explain what this feature is in oh-my-posh and what type of functionality would be needed from them so that we can support it. Can you write a detailed analysis in markdown or an explanation I can share with them, with a sequence flow, etc., explaining where it touches the current new initialization script and what would need to be added on their end if you wanted to support this?

message.content: I want to provide support for the streaming configuration in NuShell, but the problem is that NuShell does not support it. We investigated this previously. I want to create an issue on the NuGet GitHub repository asking for support. Obviously, I would need to explain what this feature is in oh-my-posh and what type of functionality would be needed from them so that we can support it. Can you write a detailed analysis in markdown or an explanation I can share with them, with a sequence flow, etc., explaining where it touches the current new initialization script and what would need to be added on their end if you wanted to support this?

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - Messages API for directly passing messages to Claude, Tool Runner (`client.beta.messages.tool_runner`) for running an agentic loop over your own tools, manual tool-use loops, Managed Agents for server-hosted agents with a managed sandbox, prompt caching, and general Anthropic SDK usage; (4) Claude Tag (Claude in Slack) - what it is, setting it up for a Slack workspace, `/install-slack-app`. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Glob, Grep, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx or .potx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. - anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.

Trigger on phrases like "Spilberg report", "Spilberg document", "make this look like Spilberg", "branded for Spilberg", or whenever the user is producing a deliverable to share with Spilberg's clients or candidates. - anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .xltx, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.thinking:
The user wants me to write a detailed markdown explanation for the Nushell team about oh-my-posh's streaming prompt feature and what Nushell would need to implement to support it. 1	package prompt
2	
3	import (
4		"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/config"
5		"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/shell"
6	)
7	
8	// TransientMarker prefixes a streamed record that contains the transient prompt
9	// rather than a primary prompt update. Shells cache such a record so rendering
10	// the transient prompt on Enter needs no additional CLI call.
11	const TransientMarker = "\x1e"
12	
13	// StreamPrimary returns a channel that yields prompt updates as segments complete.
14	//
15	// The engine + terminal package globals are not thread-safe, so at most one
16	// StreamPrimary producer goroutine may be rendering at any given time. Callers
17	// that need to interrupt an in-flight cycle (e.g. a long-lived server handling
18	// a new render request before the previous one finished) must call Abort and
19	// wait for it to return before starting a new cycle - Abort blocks until the
20	// producer goroutine has fully exited.
21	func (e *Engine) StreamPrimary() <-chan string {
22		// Initialize streaming infrastructure BEFORE launching goroutine
23		// This ensures the channel exists when segments start timing out
24		e.streamingResults = make(chan *config.Segment, 100)
25		e.allBlocks = e.Config.Blocks
26		e.abort = make(chan struct{})
27		e.done = make(chan struct{})
28	
29		out := make(chan string, 10)
30	
31		// sendRecord delivers a record unless the cycle gets aborted. A plain
32		// channel send could block forever once the buffer fills against a
33		// stalled consumer, keeping the producer from ever observing the abort
34		// and deadlocking Abort() - which waits for this goroutine to exit.
35		// Returns false when the cycle is aborted; the producer must stop then.
36		sendRecord := func(record string) bool {
37			select {
38			case out <- record:
39				return true
40			case <-e.abort:
41				return false
42			}
43		}
44	
45		// aborted reports whether the cycle has been asked to stop. Once true,
46		// the producer must not touch the engine's prompt builder or the
47		// terminal package globals again - those are shared with the next cycle.
48		aborted := func() bool {
49			select {
50			case <-e.abort:
51				return true
52			default:
53				return false
54			}
55		}
56	
57		// The transient prompt must render in the same goroutine as the primary
58		// updates: both write to the engine's prompt builder and the terminal
59		// package's global state.
60		sendTransient := func() {
61			if aborted() {
62				return
63			}
64	
65			// The zsh script caches a streamed transient record as PS1 only and
66			// resets RPROMPT (see _omp_zle-line-init in omp.zsh), so the record
67			// cannot carry a right-aligned template. Skip it to make the script
68			// fall back to the eval path which sets both PS1 and RPROMPT.
69			if e.Env.Shell() == shell.ZSH && e.Config.TransientPrompt != nil && len(e.Config.TransientPrompt.RightTemplate) != 0 {
70				return
71			}
72	
73			// The zsh script renders the transient prompt one column narrower to avoid
74			// a redundant blank line when a filler is configured and the input is empty
75			// (see _omp_zle-line-init in omp.zsh), mirror that for the streamed record.
76			if e.Env.Shell() == shell.ZSH {
77				e.rectifyTerminalWidth(-1)
78				defer e.rectifyTerminalWidth(1)
79			}
80	
81			sendRecord(TransientMarker + e.ExtraPrompt(Transient))
82		}
83	
84		go func() {
85			defer close(e.done)
86			defer close(out)
87			// Registered last so it runs first during unwinding: a panic in
88			// segment/render code then costs this one cycle instead of the whole
89			// process - which matters for the long-lived serve daemon. The closes
90			// above still run afterwards, so Abort() and the record consumer both
91			// observe a normally-ended cycle.
92			defer func() {
93				_ = recover()
94			}()
95	
96			if aborted() {
97				return
98			}
99	
100			// Render and send initial prompt with pending segments
101			if !sendRecord(e.Primary()) {
102				return
103			}
104	
105			sendTransient()
106	
107			if e.countPendingSegments() == 0 {
108				// No segment is executing in the background, so nothing can send
109				// on streamingResults after this point - safe to close.
110				close(e.streamingResults)
111				return
112			}
113	
114			// Listen for segment completions. A segment that timed out keeps
115			// executing in the background (trackPendingSegment) even after this
116			// loop returns; notifySegmentCompletion sends via select/default so
117			// it never blocks such a goroutine, but that also means
118			// streamingResults must NOT be closed here on the abort path - a
119			// stray late send on a closed channel would panic. Only close it once
120			// every pending segment has actually reported in (countPendingSegments
121			// reaches 0), which is the one path guaranteed to have no further
122			// senders. On abort, leave the channel open and let it be garbage
123			// collected once the last stray sender (and this Engine) is dropped.
124			for {
125				select {
126				case <-e.abort:
127					return
128				case _, ok := <-e.streamingResults:
129					if !ok {
130						return
131					}
132	
133					if aborted() {
134						continue
135					}
136	
137					if !sendRecord(e.renderFromBlocks()) {
138						return
139					}
140	
141					if e.countPendingSegments() == 0 {
142						// refresh the transient prompt now the context is fully resolved
143						sendTransient()
144						close(e.streamingResults)
145						return
146					}
147				}
148			}
149		}()
150	
151		return out
152	}
153	
154	// Abort signals the active StreamPrimary cycle (if any) to stop rendering and
155	// blocks until its producer goroutine has fully exited, so the caller can
156	// safely start a new cycle (on a new Engine) immediately after Abort returns.
157	// It is safe to call multiple times and safe to call when no cycle is active
158	// or the cycle has already finished on its own.
159	//
160	// Abort does not wait for segments still executing in the background after a
161	// per-segment timeout (see trackPendingSegment) - those belong to this
162	// Engine instance only and are expected to be abandoned along with it; they
163	// will report to a now-unread streamingResults channel (via
164	// notifySegmentCompletion's non-blocking send) until they finish on their own
165	// and get garbage collected with this Engine.
166	func (e *Engine) Abort() {
167		if e.abort == nil {
168			return
169		}
170	
171		select {
172		case <-e.abort:
173			// already aborted
174		default:
175			close(e.abort)
176		}
177	
178		if e.done != nil {
179			<-e.done
180		}
181	}
182	
183	// countPendingSegments counts how many segments are marked as pending
184	func (e *Engine) countPendingSegments() int {
185		count := 0
186		e.pendingSegments.Range(func(_, _ any) bool {
187			count++
188			return true
189		})
190		return count
191	}
192	
193	// renderFromBlocks re-renders the complete prompt using stored block data
194	func (e *Engine) renderFromBlocks() string {
195		// Reset prompt builder
196		e.prompt.Reset()
197		e.currentLineLength = 0
198		e.activeSegment = nil
199		e.previousActiveSegment = nil
200		e.rprompt = ""
201		e.rpromptLength = 0
202	
203		return e.primaryInternal(true)
204	}
205	
206	// trackPendingSegment continues execution for a timed-out segment in the background
207	func (e *Engine) trackPendingSegment(segment *config.Segment, done chan bool) {
208		if e.streamingResults == nil {
209			return
210		}
211	
212		// Segment is already pre-registered in pendingSegments map
213		go func() {
214			<-done
215			segment.Pending = false
216			e.notifySegmentCompletion(segment)
217		}()
218	}
219	
220	// notifySegmentCompletion sends completed segment to the streaming results channel
221	func (e *Engine) notifySegmentCompletion(segment *config.Segment) {
222		if e.streamingResults == nil {
223			return
224		}
225	
226		if _, ok := e.pendingSegments.LoadAndDelete(segment.Name()); ok {
227			select {
228			case e.streamingResults <- segment:
229				// Successfully notified consumer
230			default:
231				// Consumer not ready or already exited
232				// This can happen if segment completes after consumer finishes
233			}
234		}
235	}
236

package cli

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/cache"
	"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/prompt"
	"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/runtime"
	"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/shell"
	"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/template"

	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
)

// streamCmd represents the stream command
var streamCmd = createStreamCmd()

func init() {
	RootCmd.AddCommand(streamCmd)
}

func createStreamCmd() *cobra.Command {
	streamCmd := &cobra.Command{
		Use:   "stream",
		Short: "Stream the prompt with incremental updates",
		Long: `Stream the primary prompt with incremental updates as segments complete.
Output format: null-byte delimited prompt strings (each complete prompt separated by \0).
This allows multi-line prompts to be handled correctly.
Records prefixed with a record separator byte (\x1e) carry the transient prompt,
which shells cache so no additional CLI call is needed on line acceptance.
The shell can read records incrementally and update the display.
Command exits when all segments are resolved.`,
		Args: cobra.NoArgs,
		Run: func(_ *cobra.Command, _ []string) {
			if shellName == "" {
				shellName = shell.GENERIC
			}

			flags := &runtime.Flags{
				ConfigPath:    configFlag,
				PWD:           pwd,
				PSWD:          pswd,
				ErrorCode:     status,
				PipeStatus:    pipestatus,
				ExecutionTime: timing,
				StackCount:    stackCount,
				TerminalWidth: terminalWidth,
				Eval:          eval,
				Shell:         shellName,
				ShellVersion:  shellVersion,
				Plain:         plain,
				Type:          prompt.PRIMARY,
				Cleared:       cleared,
				NoExitCode:    noStatus,
				Column:        column,
				JobCount:      jobCount,
				IsPrimary:     true,
				Escape:        escape,
				Force:         force,
				Streaming:     true,
			}

			options := []cache.Option{}
			if saveCache {
				options = append(options, cache.Persist)
			}

			cache.Init(shellName, options...)

			eng := prompt.New(flags)

			defer func() {
				template.SaveCache()
				cache.Close()
			}()

			// Stream prompt updates
			for promptString := range eng.StreamPrimary() {
				fmt.Print(promptString)
				fmt.Print("\x00") // Null byte delimiter for multi-line prompts
			}
		},
	}

	streamCmd.Flags().StringVar(&pwd, "pwd", "", "current working directory")
	streamCmd.Flags().StringVar(&pswd, "pswd", "", "current working directory (according to pwsh)")
	streamCmd.Flags().StringVar(&shellName, "shell", "", "the shell to stream for")
	streamCmd.Flags().StringVar(&shellVersion, "shell-version", "", "the shell version")
	streamCmd.Flags().IntVar(&status, "status", 0, "last known status code")
	streamCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&noStatus, "no-status", false, "no valid status code (cancelled or no command yet)")
	streamCmd.Flags().StringVar(&pipestatus, "pipestatus", "", "the PIPESTATUS array")
	streamCmd.Flags().Float64Var(&timing, "execution-time", 0, "timing of the last command")
	streamCmd.Flags().IntVarP(&stackCount, "stack-count", "s", 0, "number of locations on the stack")
	streamCmd.Flags().IntVarP(&terminalWidth, "terminal-width", "w", 0, "width of the terminal")
	streamCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&cleared, "cleared", false, "do we have a clear terminal or not")
	streamCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&eval, "eval", false, "output the prompt for eval")
	streamCmd.Flags().IntVar(&column, "column", 0, "the column position of the cursor")
	streamCmd.Flags().IntVar(&jobCount, "job-count", 0, "number of background jobs")
	streamCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&saveCache, "save-cache", false, "save updated cache to file")
	streamCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&escape, "escape", true, "escape the ANSI sequences for the shell")
	streamCmd.Flags().BoolVarP(&force, "force", "f", false, "force rendering the segments")

	// Hide flags that are for internal use only.
	_ = streamCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("save-cache")

	return streamCmd
}

9	//go:embed scripts/omp.nu
10	var nuInit string
11	
12	func (f Features) Nu() Code {
13		switch f {
14		case Transient:
15			return `$env.TRANSIENT_PROMPT_COMMAND = {|| _omp_get_prompt transient }`
16		case Upgrade:
17			return "^$_omp_executable upgrade --auto"
18		case Notice:
19			return "^$_omp_executable notice"
20		case PromptMark, RPrompt, PoshGit, Azure, LineError, Jobs, Tooltips, FTCSMarks, CursorPositioning, Async, Streaming, KeyHandlers, VIMode:
21			fallthrough
22		default:
23			return ""
24		}
25	}
26	
27	func quoteNuStr(str string) string {
28		if str == "" {
29			return "''"
30		}
31	
32		return fmt.Sprintf(`"%s"`, strings.NewReplacer(`\`, `\\`, `"`, `\"`).Replace(str))
33	}

1	set --export --global POSH_SHELL fish
2	set --export --global POSH_SHELL_VERSION $FISH_VERSION
3	set --export --global POWERLINE_COMMAND oh-my-posh
4	set --export --global CONDA_PROMPT_MODIFIER false
5	
6	set --global _omp_tooltip_command ''
7	set --global _omp_current_rprompt ''
8	set --global _omp_transient 0
9	set --global _omp_executable ::OMP::
10	set --global _omp_cursor_positioning 0
11	set --global _omp_ftcs_marks 0
12	set --global _omp_transient_prompt 0
13	set --global _omp_transient_rprompt 0
14	set --global _omp_prompt_mark 0
15	
16	# streaming support variables
17	set --global _omp_enable_streaming 0
18	set --global _omp_streaming_pid 0
19	set --global _omp_streaming_tempfile ''
20	set --global _omp_primary_prompt ''
21	
22	# serve daemon variables
23	# A persistent `oh-my-posh serve` process renders prompts on request,
24	# replacing a process spawn per prompt with an in-memory render. Requests go
25	# through a named pipe (the only way fish can write to a running process);
26	# records flow back through a background reader into tempfiles.
27	set --global _omp_serve_pid 0
28	
29	# _omp_serve_pid holds every pid of the daemon pipeline (fish's
30	# `jobs --last --pid` lists one per stage). The first is the daemon itself
31	# and serves as the liveness proxy; kill/stop target all of them.
32	#
33	# NEVER pass a possibly-zero pid to kill: `kill -0 0` signals the caller's
34	# own process group and always succeeds.
35	function _omp_serve_alive
36	    test -n "$_omp_serve_pid[1]" -a "$_omp_serve_pid[1]" -gt 0 2>/dev/null
37	    and kill -0 $_omp_serve_pid[1] 2>/dev/null
38	end
39	set --global _omp_serve_fifo ''
40	set --global _omp_serve_tempfile ''
41	set --global _omp_serve_cycle 0
42	set --global _omp_serve_failures 0
43	
44	# disable all known python virtual environment prompts
45	set --global VIRTUAL_ENV_DISABLE_PROMPT 1
46	set --global PYENV_VIRTUALENV_DISABLE_PROMPT 1
47	
48	# We use this to avoid unnecessary CLI calls for prompt repaint.
49	set --global _omp_new_prompt 1
50	
51	function _omp_set_cursor_position
52	    # not supported in Midnight Commander
53	    # see https://github.com/JanDeDobbeleer/oh-my-posh/issues/3415
54	    if test "$_omp_cursor_positioning" = 0; or set --query MC_SID
55	        return
56	    end
57	
58	    set --local oldstty (stty -g </dev/tty)
59	    stty raw -echo min 1 </dev/tty
60	
61	    set --local pos ''
62	    printf '\e[6n' >/dev/tty
63	    while true
64	        read --null --nchars 1 --local ch </dev/tty
65	        set pos $pos$ch
66	        string match -q 'R' $ch; and break
67	    end
68	
69	    stty $oldstty </dev/tty
70	
71	    set --local parts (string match -gr '\[(\d+);(\d+)R' $pos)
72	    set --export --global POSH_CURSOR_LINE $parts[1]
73	    set --export --global POSH_CURSOR_COLUMN $parts[2]
74	end
75	
76	# template function for context loading
77	function set_poshcontext
78	    return
79	end
80	
81	# cleanup stream resources
82	function _omp_cleanup_stream
83	    # kill the background pipeline if running; _omp_streaming_pid holds one
84	    # pid per pipeline stage, the first serves as the liveness proxy
85	    if test -n "$_omp_streaming_pid[1]" -a "$_omp_streaming_pid[1]" -gt 0 2>/dev/null
86	        kill $_omp_streaming_pid 2>/dev/null
87	        set --global _omp_streaming_pid 0
88	    end
89	    # remove temp file
90	    if test -n "$_omp_streaming_tempfile"
91	        rm -f "$_omp_streaming_tempfile" 2>/dev/null
92	    end
93	end
94	
95	# shell exit handler
96	function _omp_exit_handler --on-event fish_exit
97	    _omp_serve_quit
98	    _omp_cleanup_stream
99	    # the transient prompt cache outlives stream cleanup by design, remove it on exit
100	    if test -n "$_omp_streaming_tempfile"
101	        rm -f "$_omp_streaming_tempfile.transient" 2>/dev/null
102	    end
103	end
104	
105	# streaming background reader: reads null-delimited prompts and signals parent.
106	#
107	# IMPORTANT: this must run as an external `fish -c` process. fish does NOT
108	# fork a backgrounded pipeline stage that is a function - it runs in the main
109	# shell and blocks it. An external process pipeline forks properly, hence
110	# tempfiles + SIGUSR1 instead of variables.
111	set --global _omp_streaming_reader_script '
112	set parent_pid $argv[1]
113	set tempfile $argv[2]
114	set count 0
115	set marker (printf "\x1e")
116	while read --null prompt
117	    # a record prefixed with U+001E carries the transient prompt: cache it
118	    # for the key handlers so rendering the transient prompt needs no CLI call
119	    if test (string sub --length 1 -- $prompt | string collect) = "$marker"
120	        printf "%s" (string sub --start 2 -- $prompt | string collect) >"$tempfile.transient"
121	        continue
122	    end
123	    # write to temp file (atomic via printf)
124	    printf "%s" "$prompt" >$tempfile
125	    # signal parent for updates after first prompt (index > 0)
126	    if test $count -gt 0
127	        kill -SIGUSR1 $parent_pid 2>/dev/null
128	    end
129	    set count (math $count + 1)
130	end
131	'
132	
133	# signal handler: called when streaming process has new prompt
134	function _omp_streaming_handler --on-signal SIGUSR1
135	    # only process if streaming is active
136	    if test $_omp_enable_streaming -eq 0
137	        return
138	    end
139	
140	    # serve path: records carry a cycle id prefix; discard stale ones and
141	    # skip repaints for unchanged content (the synchronously consumed first
142	    # record also signals)
143	    if test -n "$_omp_serve_tempfile" -a -f "$_omp_serve_tempfile"; and _omp_serve_alive
144	        set --local us (printf '\x1f')
145	        set --local record (cat "$_omp_serve_tempfile" | string collect)
146	        set --local id (string split --max 1 -- $us $record)[1]
147	        if test "$id" != "$_omp_serve_cycle"
148	            return
149	        end
150	
151	        set --local payload (string replace -- "$id$us" '' $record | string collect)
152	        if test "$payload" = "$_omp_primary_prompt"
153	            return
154	        end
155	
156	        set --global _omp_primary_prompt $payload
157	        set --global _omp_current_prompt "$payload"
158	        commandline --function repaint
159	        return
160	    end
161	
162	    if test -z "$_omp_streaming_tempfile" -o ! -f "$_omp_streaming_tempfile"
163	        return
164	    end
165	
166	    # read updated prompt from temp file
167	    set --global _omp_primary_prompt (cat "$_omp_streaming_tempfile")
168	    set --global _omp_current_prompt "$_omp_primary_prompt"
169	
170	    # trigger repaint
171	    commandline --function repaint
172	end
173	
174	# start oh-my-posh stream process, block until first prompt arrives
175	function _omp_start_streaming
176	    # cleanup any prior stream
177	    _omp_cleanup_stream
178	
179	    # determine temp file location
180	    set --local tmpdir $TMPDIR
181	    if test -z "$tmpdir"
182	        set tmpdir /tmp
183	    end
184	    set --global _omp_streaming_tempfile "$tmpdir/omp-fish-$fish_pid.txt"
185	    # also invalidate the previous cycle's transient prompt
186	    rm -f "$_omp_streaming_tempfile" "$_omp_streaming_tempfile.transient" 2>/dev/null
187	
188	    # build stream command with all context
189	    set --local stream_cmd $_omp_executable stream \
190	        --save-cache \
191	        --shell=fish \
192	        --shell-version=$FISH_VERSION \
193	        --status=$_omp_status \
194	        --pipestatus="$_omp_pipestatus" \
195	        --no-status=$_omp_no_status \
196	        --execution-time=$_omp_execution_time \
197	        --stack-count=$_omp_stack_count
198	
199	    # start background reader process
200	    $stream_cmd | fish --no-config -c $_omp_streaming_reader_script $fish_pid "$_omp_streaming_tempfile" &
201	    # `jobs --last --pid` prints a header line plus one pid per pipeline
202	    # stage (fish 4.x) - keep only the numbers
203	    set --global _omp_streaming_pid (jobs --last --pid | string match --regex '^\d+$')
204	
205	    # keep the stream pipeline out of job notifications
206	    disown $_omp_streaming_pid 2>/dev/null
207	
208	    # block until first prompt arrives (mirrors zsh's blocking read)
209	    set --local timeout 5000
210	    set --local elapsed 0
211	    while not test -s "$_omp_streaming_tempfile"
212	        sleep 0.01
213	        set elapsed (math $elapsed + 10)
214	        if test $elapsed -ge $timeout
215	            # timeout - cleanup and return failure
216	            _omp_cleanup_stream
217	            return 1
218	        end
219	    end
220	
221	    # read first prompt
222	    set --global _omp_primary_prompt (cat "$_omp_streaming_tempfile")
223	    set --global _omp_current_prompt "$_omp_primary_prompt"
224	
225	    return 0
226	end
227	
228	# === serve daemon ===
229	
230	# background reader for the daemon's records: "<id>\x1f<payload>\0", where a
231	# payload prefixed with \x1e carries the transient prompt.
232	#
233	# IMPORTANT: this must run as an external `fish -c` process. fish does NOT
234	# fork a backgrounded pipeline stage that is a function - it runs in the main
235	# shell and blocks it. An external process pipeline forks properly, hence
236	# tempfiles + SIGUSR1 instead of variables.
237	set --global _omp_serve_reader_script '
238	set parent_pid $argv[1]
239	set tempfile $argv[2]
240	set marker (printf "\x1e")
241	while read --null record
242	    set m (string match --regex -- "^(\d+)\x1f" $record)
243	    if test (count $m) -ne 2
244	        continue
245	    end
246	    set prefix_len (string length -- "$m[1]")
247	    set payload (string sub --start (math $prefix_len + 1) -- $record | string collect)
248	    # transient record: cache for the key handlers, never repaint
249	    if test (string sub --length 1 -- $payload | string collect) = "$marker"
250	        printf "%s" (string sub --start 2 -- $payload | string collect) >"$tempfile.transient"
251	        continue
252	    end
253	    # keep the id with the payload so consumers can discard stale records
254	    printf "%s\x1f%s" $m[2] $payload >$tempfile
255	    # wake the parent; its SIGUSR1 handler dedupes unchanged content, so
256	    # signaling the synchronously consumed first record is harmless
257	    kill -SIGUSR1 $parent_pid 2>/dev/null
258	end
259	'
260	
261	function _omp_serve_stop
262	    if test -n "$_omp_serve_pid[1]" -a "$_omp_serve_pid[1]" -gt 0 2>/dev/null
263	        kill $_omp_serve_pid 2>/dev/null
264	    end
265	    set --global _omp_serve_pid 0
266	
267	    if test -n "$_omp_serve_fifo"
268	        rm -f "$_omp_serve_fifo" 2>/dev/null
269	        set --global _omp_serve_fifo ''
270	    end
271	    # the tempfiles survive on purpose: the .transient cache must outlive the
272	    # cycle so the queued transient repaint can still use it
273	end
274	
275	function _omp_serve_start
276	    _omp_serve_stop
277	
278	    set --local tmpdir $TMPDIR
279	    if test -z "$tmpdir"
280	        set tmpdir /tmp
281	    end
282	
283	    set --global _omp_serve_fifo "$tmpdir/omp-serve-$fish_pid.req"
284	    set --global _omp_serve_tempfile "$tmpdir/omp-serve-$fish_pid.txt"
285	    rm -f "$_omp_serve_fifo" "$_omp_serve_tempfile" "$_omp_serve_tempfile.transient" 2>/dev/null
286	
287	    if not mkfifo -m 600 "$_omp_serve_fifo" 2>/dev/null
288	        return 1
289	    end
290	
291	    # The daemon opens the fifo itself read-write, so our open-write-close
292	    # per request never EOFs it. Its stderr must never reach the terminal
293	    # (a Go panic would corrupt the display), and it self-terminates when its
294	    # stdout reader disappears (broken pipe on the next record write).
295	    $_omp_executable serve --shell=fish --request-pipe "$_omp_serve_fifo" 2>/dev/null | fish --no-config -c $_omp_serve_reader_script $fish_pid "$_omp_serve_tempfile" &
296	    # `jobs --last --pid` prints a header line plus one pid per pipeline
297	    # stage (fish 4.x) - keep only the numbers
298	    set --global _omp_serve_pid (jobs --last --pid | string match --regex '^\d+$')
299	
300	    if not _omp_serve_alive
301	        _omp_serve_stop
302	        return 1
303	    end
304	
305	    # keep the daemon pipeline out of job notifications
306	    disown $_omp_serve_pid 2>/dev/null
307	
308	    return 0
309	end
310	
311	# JSON string escaping with builtins. Joining the result with '' drops any
312	# embedded newlines - the values we send (paths, POSH_* variables) never
313	# legitimately contain them, and a raw newline would break the
314	# line-delimited request protocol.
315	function _omp_serve_escape
316	    string join '' -- (string replace -a -- '\\' '\\\\' "$argv" | string replace -a -- '"' '\\"' | string replace -a -- \t '\\t')
317	end
318	
319	function _omp_serve_env_json
320	    set --local parts
321	
322	    set --append parts '"PATH":"'(_omp_serve_escape (string join ':' -- $PATH))'"'
323	
324	    # forward every exported POSH_* variable plus the virtual-env markers;
325	    # the daemon's environment is otherwise frozen at its start
326	    for name in (set --names)
327	        if string match -q 'POSH_*' -- $name; and set -qx $name
328	            set --append parts '"'$name'":"'(_omp_serve_escape "$$name")'"'
329	        end
330	    end
331	
332	    for name in VIRTUAL_ENV CONDA_PROMPT_MODIFIER
333	        if set -q $name
334	            set --append parts '"'$name'":"'(_omp_serve_escape "$$name")'"'
335	        end
336	    end
337	
338	    echo -n '{'(string join ',' -- $parts)'}'
339	end
340	
341	function _omp_serve_request
342	    set --global _omp_serve_cycle (math $_omp_serve_cycle + 1)
343	    rm -f "$_omp_serve_tempfile" "$_omp_serve_tempfile.transient" 2>/dev/null
344	
345	    set --local exec_time $_omp_execution_time
346	    if test -z "$exec_time"
347	        set exec_time 0
348	    end
349	
350	    set --local width $COLUMNS
351	    if test -z "$width"
352	        set width 0
353	    end
354	
355	    set --local cleared $_omp_cleared
356	    if test -z "$cleared"
357	        set cleared false
358	    end
359	
360	    set --local json '{"command":"render","id":'$_omp_serve_cycle',"shell":"fish","shell-version":"'$FISH_VERSION'","status":'$_omp_status',"pipestatus":"'"$_omp_pipestatus"'","no-status":'$_omp_no_status',"execution-time":'$exec_time',"stack-count":'(count $dirstack)',"terminal-width":'$width',"cleared":'$cleared',"pwd":"'(_omp_serve_escape $PWD)'","env":'(_omp_serve_env_json)'}'
361	
362	    # a fifo write with no reader blocks forever - only write while the
363	    # daemon pipeline is alive (the daemon holds the fifo open read-write,
364	    # so a live daemon can never block us)
365	    if not _omp_serve_alive
366	        return 1
367	    end
368	
369	    echo $json >"$_omp_serve_fifo" 2>/dev/null
370	end
371	
372	# poll the tempfile until it holds this cycle's primary record
373	function _omp_serve_read_primary
374	    set --local us (printf '\x1f')
375	    set --local timeout 2000
376	    set --local elapsed 0
377	
378	    while test $elapsed -lt $timeout
379	        if test -s "$_omp_serve_tempfile"
380	            set --local record (cat "$_omp_serve_tempfile" | string collect)
381	            set --local id (string split --max 1 -- $us $record)[1]
382	            if test "$id" = "$_omp_serve_cycle"
383	                string replace -- "$id$us" '' $record | string collect
384	                return 0
385	            end
386	        end
387	        sleep 0.01
388	        set elapsed (math $elapsed + 10)
389	    end
390	
391	    return 1
392	end
393	
394	# renders the primary prompt through the daemon; returns nonzero on failure,
395	# in which case the caller falls back to the per-prompt stream. The transient
396	# prompt lands in "$_omp_serve_tempfile.transient" via the reader.
397	function _omp_serve_render
398	    if not _omp_serve_alive
399	        if not _omp_serve_start
400	            set --global _omp_serve_failures (math $_omp_serve_failures + 1)
401	            return 1
402	        end
403	    end
404	
405	    if not _omp_serve_request
406	        # the daemon died since the last prompt - restart it once
407	        if not _omp_serve_start; or not _omp_serve_request
408	            set --global _omp_serve_failures (math $_omp_serve_failures + 1)
409	            _omp_serve_stop
410	            return 1
411	        end
412	    end
413	
414	    set --local prompt (_omp_serve_read_primary)
415	    if test -z "$prompt"
416	        set --global _omp_serve_failures (math $_omp_serve_failures + 1)
417	        _omp_serve_stop
418	        return 1
419	    end
420	
421	    set --global _omp_primary_prompt $prompt
422	    set --global _omp_current_prompt "$prompt"
423	    return 0
424	end
425	
426	function _omp_serve_abort
427	    if test -n "$_omp_serve_fifo"; and _omp_serve_alive
428	        echo '{"command":"abort"}' >"$_omp_serve_fifo" 2>/dev/null
429	    end
430	end
431	
432	function _omp_serve_quit
433	    if test -n "$_omp_serve_fifo"; and _omp_serve_alive
434	        echo '{"command":"quit"}' >"$_omp_serve_fifo" 2>/dev/null
435	    end
436	    _omp_serve_stop
437	    if test -n "$_omp_serve_tempfile"
438	        rm -f "$_omp_serve_tempfile" "$_omp_serve_tempfile.transient" 2>/dev/null
439	        set --global _omp_serve_tempfile ''
440	    end
441	end
442	
443	function _omp_get_prompt
444	    if test (count $argv) -eq 0
445	        return
446	    end
447	    $_omp_executable print $argv[1] \
448	        --save-cache \
449	        --shell=fish \
450	        --shell-version=$FISH_VERSION \
451	        --status=$_omp_status \
452	        --pipestatus="$_omp_pipestatus" \
453	        --no-status=$_omp_no_status \
454	        --execution-time=$_omp_execution_time \
455	        --stack-count=$_omp_stack_count \
456	        $argv[2..]
457	end
458	
459	# NOTE: Input function calls via `commandline --function` are put into a queue and will not be executed until an outer regular function returns. See https://fishshell.com/docs/current/cmds/commandline.html.
460	
461	function fish_prompt
462	    set --local omp_status_temp $status
463	    set --local omp_pipestatus_temp $pipestatus
464	    # clear from cursor to end of screen as
465	    # commandline --function repaint does not do this
466	    # see https://github.com/fish-shell/fish-shell/issues/8418
467	    printf \e\[0J
468	    if test "$_omp_transient" = 1
469	        # prefer the transient prompt rendered ahead of time by the serve
470	        # daemon or the streaming process, saves a CLI call
471	        if test -n "$_omp_serve_tempfile"; and test -s "$_omp_serve_tempfile.transient"
472	            cat "$_omp_serve_tempfile.transient"
473	        else if test $_omp_enable_streaming -eq 1; and test -n "$_omp_streaming_tempfile"; and test -s "$_omp_streaming_tempfile.transient"
474	            cat "$_omp_streaming_tempfile.transient"
475	        else
476	            _omp_get_prompt transient
477	        end
478	        return
479	    end
480	    if test "$_omp_new_prompt" = 0
481	        echo -n "$_omp_current_prompt"
482	        return
483	    end
484	    set --global _omp_status $omp_status_temp
485	    set --global _omp_pipestatus $omp_pipestatus_temp
486	    set --global _omp_no_status false
487	    set --global _omp_execution_time "$CMD_DURATION$cmd_duration"
488	    set --global _omp_stack_count (count $dirstack)
489	
490	    # check if variable set, < 3.2 case
491	    if set --query _omp_last_command && test -z "$_omp_last_command"
492	        set _omp_execution_time 0
493	        set _omp_no_status true
494	    end
495	
496	    # works with fish >=3.2
497	    if set --query _omp_last_status_generation && test "$_omp_last_status_generation" = "$status_generation"
498	        set _omp_execution_time 0
499	        set _omp_no_status true
500	    else if test -z "$_omp_last_status_generation"
501	        # first execution - $status_generation is 0, $_omp_last_status_generation is empty
502	        set _omp_no_status true
503	    end
504	
505	    if set --query status_generation
506	        set --global _omp_last_status_generation $status_generation
507	    end
508	
509	    set_poshcontext
510	    _omp_set_cursor_position
511	
512	    # validate if the user cleared the screen; global so the serve daemon
513	    # request builder sees it too
514	    set --global _omp_cleared false
515	    set --local last_command (history search --max 1)
516	
517	    if test "$last_command" = clear
518	        set --global _omp_cleared true
519	    end
520	
521	    if test $_omp_prompt_mark = 1
522	        iterm2_prompt_mark
523	    end
524	
525	    # === STREAMING PATH ===
526	    if test $_omp_enable_streaming -eq 1
527	        # serve daemon: persistent process, no spawn per prompt. After three
528	        # failures the daemon is left alone for the session and every prompt
529	        # takes the per-prompt stream below instead.
530	        if test $_omp_serve_failures -lt 3; and _omp_serve_render
531	            echo -n "$_omp_current_prompt"
532	            return
533	        end
534	
535	        # per-prompt stream (also the serve fallback; blocks until first prompt arrives)
536	        if _omp_start_streaming
537	            # _omp_current_prompt already set by _omp_start_streaming
538	            echo -n "$_omp_current_prompt"
539	            return
540	        end
541	        # fall through to sync on failure
542	    end
543	
544	    # === FALLBACK PATH ===
545	    # The prompt is saved for possible reuse, typically a repaint after clearing the screen buffer.
546	    set --global _omp_current_prompt (_omp_get_prompt primary --cleared=$_omp_cleared | string join \n | string collect)
547	
548	    echo -n "$_omp_current_prompt"
549	end
550	
551	function fish_right_prompt
552	    if test "$_omp_transient" = 1
553	        set --global _omp_transient 0
554	
555	        if test $_omp_transient_rprompt = 1
556	            _omp_get_prompt transient-right
557	        end
558	
559	        return
560	    end
561	
562	    # Repaint an existing right prompt.
563	    if test "$_omp_new_prompt" = 0
564	        echo -n "$_omp_current_rprompt"
565	        return
566	    end
567	
568	    set --global _omp_new_prompt 0
569	    set --global _omp_current_rprompt (_omp_get_prompt right | string join '')
570	
571	    echo -n "$_omp_current_rprompt"
572	end
573	
574	function _omp_postexec --on-event fish_postexec
575	    # works with fish <3.2
576	    # pre and postexec not fired for empty command in fish >=3.2
577	    set --global _omp_last_command $argv
578	end
579	
580	function _omp_preexec --on-event fish_preexec
581	    if test $_omp_ftcs_marks != 1
582	        return
583	    end
584	
585	    if test -n "$argv"
586	        # advertise the command line via kitty's cmdline_url= extension
587	        echo -ne "\e]133;C;cmdline_url="(string escape --style=url -- "$argv")"\a"
588	        return
589	    end
590	
591	    echo -ne "\e]133;C\a"
592	end
593	
594	# perform cleanup so a new initialization in current session works
595	if bind \r --user 2>/dev/null | string match -qe _omp_enter_key_handler
596	    bind -e \r -M default
597	    bind -e \r -M insert
598	    bind -e \r -M visual
599	end
600	
601	if bind \n --user 2>/dev/null | string match -qe _omp_enter_key_handler
602	    bind -e \n -M default
603	    bind -e \n -M insert
604	    bind -e \n -M visual
605	end
606	
607	if bind \cc --user 2>/dev/null | string match -qe _omp_ctrl_c_key_handler
608	    bind -e \cc -M default
609	    bind -e \cc -M insert
610	    bind -e \cc -M visual
611	end
612	
613	if bind \x20 --user 2>/dev/null | string match -qe _omp_space_key_handler
614	    bind -e \x20 -M default
615	    bind -e \x20 -M insert
616	end
617	
618	# tooltip
619	
620	function _omp_space_key_handler
621	    commandline --function expand-abbr
622	    commandline --insert ' '
623	
624	    # Get the first word of command line as tip.
625	    set --local tooltip_command (commandline --current-buffer | string trim -l | string split --allow-empty -f1 ' ' | string collect)
626	
627	    # Ignore an empty/repeated tooltip command.
628	    if test -z "$tooltip_command" || test "$tooltip_command" = "$_omp_tooltip_command"
629	        return
630	    end
631	
632	    set _omp_tooltip_command $tooltip_command
633	    set --local tooltip_prompt (_omp_get_prompt tooltip --command=$_omp_tooltip_command | string join '')
634	
635	    if test -z "$tooltip_prompt"
636	        return
637	    end
638	
639	    # Save the tooltip prompt to avoid unnecessary CLI calls.
640	    set _omp_current_rprompt $tooltip_prompt
641	    commandline --function repaint
642	end
643	
644	function _omp_backspace_key_handler
645	    commandline --function backward-delete-char
646	
647	    if test -z "$_omp_tooltip_command"
648	        return
649	    end
650	
651	    set --local current_command (commandline --current-buffer | string trim -l | string split --allow-empty -f1 ' ' | string collect)
652	
653	    if test "$current_command" = "$_omp_tooltip_command"
654	        return
655	    end
656	
657	    set _omp_tooltip_command "$current_command"
658	    set _omp_current_rprompt (_omp_get_prompt tooltip --command="$current_command" | string join '')
659	    commandline --function repaint
660	end
661	
662	function enable_poshtooltips
663	    bind \x20 _omp_space_key_handler -M default
664	    bind \x20 _omp_space_key_handler -M insert
665	    bind \x7f _omp_backspace_key_handler -M default
666	    bind \x7f _omp_backspace_key_handler -M insert
667	end
668	
669	# transient prompt
670	
671	function _omp_enter_key_handler
672	    if commandline --paging-mode
673	        set --global _omp_new_prompt 1
674	        set --global _omp_tooltip_command ''
675	
676	        # stop prompt updates before executing: kill the per-prompt stream,
677	        # tell the daemon to abort its in-flight cycle (the daemon lives on)
678	        _omp_cleanup_stream
679	        _omp_serve_abort
680	
681	        if test $_omp_transient_prompt = 1
682	            set --global _omp_transient 1
683	            commandline --function repaint
684	        end
685	
686	        commandline --function execute
687	        return
688	    end
689	
690	    if commandline --is-valid || test -z (commandline --current-buffer | string trim -l | string collect)
691	        set --global _omp_new_prompt 1
692	        set --global _omp_tooltip_command ''
693	
694	        # stop prompt updates before executing: kill the per-prompt stream,
695	        # tell the daemon to abort its in-flight cycle (the daemon lives on)
696	        _omp_cleanup_stream
697	        _omp_serve_abort
698	
699	        if test $_omp_transient_prompt = 1
700	            set --global _omp_transient 1
701	            commandline --function repaint
702	        end
703	    end
704	
705	    commandline --function execute
706	end
707	
708	function _omp_ctrl_c_key_handler
709	    if test -z (commandline --current-buffer | string collect)
710	        return
711	    end
712	
713	    # Render a transient prompt on Ctrl-C with non-empty command line buffer.
714	    set --global _omp_new_prompt 1
715	    set --global _omp_tooltip_command ''
716	
717	    # stop prompt updates before canceling (the daemon lives on)
718	    _omp_cleanup_stream
719	    _omp_serve_abort
720	
721	    if test $_omp_transient_prompt = 1
722	        set --global _omp_transient 1
723	        commandline --function repaint
724	    end
725	
726	    commandline --function cancel-commandline
727	    commandline --function repaint
728	end
729	
730	bind \r _omp_enter_key_handler -M default
731	bind \r _omp_enter_key_handler -M insert
732	bind \r _omp_enter_key_handler -M visual
733	bind \n _omp_enter_key_handler -M default
734	bind \n _omp_enter_key_handler -M insert
735	bind \n _omp_enter_key_handler -M visual
736	bind \cc _omp_ctrl_c_key_handler -M default
737	bind \cc _omp_ctrl_c_key_handler -M insert
738	bind \cc _omp_ctrl_c_key_handler -M visual
739	
740	# legacy functions
741	function enable_poshtransientprompt
742	    return
743	end
744	
745	# This can be called by user whenever re-rendering is required.
746	function omp_repaint_prompt
747	    set --global _omp_new_prompt 1
748	    commandline --function repaint
749	end

file.content:
set --export --global POSH_SHELL fish
set --export --global POSH_SHELL_VERSION $FISH_VERSION
set --export --global POWERLINE_COMMAND oh-my-posh
set --export --global CONDA_PROMPT_MODIFIER false

set --global _omp_tooltip_command ''
set --global _omp_current_rprompt ''
set --global _omp_transient 0
set --global _omp_executable ::OMP::
set --global _omp_cursor_positioning 0
set --global _omp_ftcs_marks 0
set --global _omp_transient_prompt 0
set --global _omp_transient_rprompt 0
set --global _omp_prompt_mark 0

# streaming support variables
set --global _omp_enable_streaming 0
set --global _omp_streaming_pid 0
set --global _omp_streaming_tempfile ''
set --global _omp_primary_prompt ''

# serve daemon variables
# A persistent `oh-my-posh serve` process renders prompts on request,
# replacing a process spawn per prompt with an in-memory render. Requests go
# through a named pipe (the only way fish can write to a running process);
# records flow back through a background reader into tempfiles.
set --global _omp_serve_pid 0

# _omp_serve_pid holds every pid of the daemon pipeline (fish's
# `jobs --last --pid` lists one per stage). The first is the daemon itself
# and serves as the liveness proxy; kill/stop target all of them.
#
# NEVER pass a possibly-zero pid to kill: `kill -0 0` signals the caller's
# own process group and always succeeds.
function _omp_serve_alive
    test -n "$_omp_serve_pid[1]" -a "$_omp_serve_pid[1]" -gt 0 2>/dev/null
    and kill -0 $_omp_serve_pid[1] 2>/dev/null
end
set --global _omp_serve_fifo ''
set --global _omp_serve_tempfile ''
set --global _omp_serve_cycle 0
set --global _omp_serve_failures 0

# disable all known python virtual environment prompts
set --global VIRTUAL_ENV_DISABLE_PROMPT 1
set --global PYENV_VIRTUALENV_DISABLE_PROMPT 1

# We use this to avoid unnecessary CLI calls for prompt repaint.
set --global _omp_new_prompt 1

function _omp_set_cursor_position
    # not supported in Midnight Commander
    # see https://github.com/JanDeDobbeleer/oh-my-posh/issues/3415
    if test "$_omp_cursor_positioning" = 0; or set --query MC_SID
        return
    end

    set --local oldstty (stty -g </dev/tty)
    stty raw -echo min 1 </dev/tty

    set --local pos ''
    printf '\e[6n' >/dev/tty
    while true
        read --null --nchars 1 --local ch </dev/tty
        set pos $pos$ch
        string match -q 'R' $ch; and break
    end

    stty $oldstty </dev/tty

    set --local parts (string match -gr '\[(\d+);(\d+)R' $pos)
    set --export --global POSH_CURSOR_LINE $parts[1]
    set --export --global POSH_CURSOR_COLUMN $parts[2]
end

# template function for context loading
function set_poshcontext
    return
end

# cleanup stream resources
function _omp_cleanup_stream
    # kill the background pipeline if running; _omp_streaming_pid holds one
    # pid per pipeline stage, the first serves as the liveness proxy
    if test -n "$_omp_streaming_pid[1]" -a "$_omp_streaming_pid[1]" -gt 0 2>/dev/null
        kill $_omp_streaming_pid 2>/dev/null
        set --global _omp_streaming_pid 0
    end
    # remove temp file
    if test -n "$_omp_streaming_tempfile"
        rm -f "$_omp_streaming_tempfile" 2>/dev/null
    end
end

# shell exit handler
function _omp_exit_handler --on-event fish_exit
    _omp_serve_quit
    _omp_cleanup_stream
    # the transient prompt cache outlives stream cleanup by design, remove it on exit
    if test -n "$_omp_streaming_tempfile"
        rm -f "$_omp_streaming_tempfile.transient" 2>/dev/null
    end
end

# streaming background reader: reads null-delimited prompts and signals parent.
#
# IMPORTANT: this must run as an external `fish -c` process. fish does NOT
# fork a backgrounded pipeline stage that is a function - it runs in the main
# shell and blocks it. An external process pipeline forks properly, hence
# tempfiles + SIGUSR1 instead of variables.
set --global _omp_streaming_reader_script '
set parent_pid $argv[1]
set tempfile $argv[2]
set count 0
set marker (printf "\x1e")
while read --null prompt
    # a record prefixed with U+001E carries the transient prompt: cache it
    # for the key handlers so rendering the transient prompt needs no CLI call
    if test (string sub --length 1 -- $prompt | string collect) = "$marker"
        printf "%s" (string sub --start 2 -- $prompt | string collect) >"$tempfile.transient"
        continue
    end
    # write to temp file (atomic via printf)
    printf "%s" "$prompt" >$tempfile
    # signal parent for updates after first prompt (index > 0)
    if test $count -gt 0
        kill -SIGUSR1 $parent_pid 2>/dev/null
    end
    set count (math $count + 1)
end
'

# signal handler: called when streaming process has new prompt
function _omp_streaming_handler --on-signal SIGUSR1
    # only process if streaming is active
    if test $_omp_enable_streaming -eq 0
        return
    end

    # serve path: records carry a cycle id prefix; discard stale ones and
    # skip repaints for unchanged content (the synchronously consumed first
    # record also signals)
    if test -n "$_omp_serve_tempfile" -a -f "$_omp_serve_tempfile"; and _omp_serve_alive
        set --local us (printf '\x1f')
        set --local record (cat "$_omp_serve_tempfile" | string collect)
        set --local id (string split --max 1 -- $us $record)[1]
        if test "$id" != "$_omp_serve_cycle"
            return
        end

        set --local payload (string replace -- "$id$us" '' $record | string collect)
        if test "$payload" = "$_omp_primary_prompt"
            return
        end

        set --global _omp_primary_prompt $payload
        set --global _omp_current_prompt "$payload"
        commandline --function repaint
        return
    end

    if test -z "$_omp_streaming_tempfile" -o ! -f "$_omp_streaming_tempfile"
        return
    end

    # read updated prompt from temp file
    set --global _omp_primary_prompt (cat "$_omp_streaming_tempfile")
    set --global _omp_current_prompt "$_omp_primary_prompt"

    # trigger repaint
    commandline --function repaint
end

# start oh-my-posh stream process, block until first prompt arrives
function _omp_start_streaming
    # cleanup any prior stream
    _omp_cleanup_stream

    # determine temp file location
    set --local tmpdir $TMPDIR
    if test -z "$tmpdir"
        set tmpdir /tmp
    end
    set --global _omp_streaming_tempfile "$tmpdir/omp-fish-$fish_pid.txt"
    # also invalidate the previous cycle's transient prompt
    rm -f "$_omp_streaming_tempfile" "$_omp_streaming_tempfile.transient" 2>/dev/null

    # build stream command with all context
    set --local stream_cmd $_omp_executable stream \
        --save-cache \
        --shell=fish \
        --shell-version=$FISH_VERSION \
        --status=$_omp_status \
        --pipestatus="$_omp_pipestatus" \
        --no-status=$_omp_no_status \
        --execution-time=$_omp_execution_time \
        --stack-count=$_omp_stack_count

    # start background reader process
    $stream_cmd | fish --no-config -c $_omp_streaming_reader_script $fish_pid "$_omp_streaming_tempfile" &
    # `jobs --last --pid` prints a header line plus one pid per pipeline
    # stage (fish 4.x) - keep only the numbers
    set --global _omp_streaming_pid (jobs --last --pid | string match --regex '^\d+$')

    # keep the stream pipeline out of job notifications
    disown $_omp_streaming_pid 2>/dev/null

    # block until first prompt arrives (mirrors zsh's blocking read)
    set --local timeout 5000
    set --local elapsed 0
    while not test -s "$_omp_streaming_tempfile"
        sleep 0.01
        set elapsed (math $elapsed + 10)
        if test $elapsed -ge $timeout
            # timeout - cleanup and return failure
            _omp_cleanup_stream
            return 1
        end
    end

    # read first prompt
    set --global _omp_primary_prompt (cat "$_omp_streaming_tempfile")
    set --global _omp_current_prompt "$_omp_primary_prompt"

    return 0
end

# === serve daemon ===

# background reader for the daemon's records: "<id>\x1f<payload>\0", where a
# payload prefixed with \x1e carries the transient prompt.
#
# IMPORTANT: this must run as an external `fish -c` process. fish does NOT
# fork a backgrounded pipeline stage that is a function - it runs in the main
# shell and blocks it. An external process pipeline forks properly, hence
# tempfiles + SIGUSR1 instead of variables.
set --global _omp_serve_reader_script '
set parent_pid $argv[1]
set tempfile $argv[2]
set marker (printf "\x1e")
while read --null record
    set m (string match --regex -- "^(\d+)\x1f" $record)
    if test (count $m) -ne 2
        continue
    end
    set prefix_len (string length -- "$m[1]")
    set payload (string sub --start (math $prefix_len + 1) -- $record | string collect)
    # transient record: cache for the key handlers, never repaint
    if test (string sub --length 1 -- $payload | string collect) = "$marker"
        printf "%s" (string sub --start 2 -- $payload | string collect) >"$tempfile.transient"
        continue
    end
    # keep the id with the payload so consumers can discard stale records
    printf "%s\x1f%s" $m[2] $payload >$tempfile
    # wake the parent; its SIGUSR1 handler dedupes unchanged content, so
    # signaling the synchronously consumed first record is harmless
    kill -SIGUSR1 $parent_pid 2>/dev/null
end
'

function _omp_serve_stop
    if test -n "$_omp_serve_pid[1]" -a "$_omp_serve_pid[1]" -gt 0 2>/dev/null
        kill $_omp_serve_pid 2>/dev/null
    end
    set --global _omp_serve_pid 0

    if test -n "$_omp_serve_fifo"
        rm -f "$_omp_serve_fifo" 2>/dev/null
        set --global _omp_serve_fifo ''
    end
    # the tempfiles survive on purpose: the .transient cache must outlive the
    # cycle so the queued transient repaint can still use it
end

function _omp_serve_start
    _omp_serve_stop

    set --local tmpdir $TMPDIR
    if test -z "$tmpdir"
        set tmpdir /tmp
    end

    set --global _omp_serve_fifo "$tmpdir/omp-serve-$fish_pid.req"
    set --global _omp_serve_tempfile "$tmpdir/omp-serve-$fish_pid.txt"
    rm -f "$_omp_serve_fifo" "$_omp_serve_tempfile" "$_omp_serve_tempfile.transient" 2>/dev/null

    if not mkfifo -m 600 "$_omp_serve_fifo" 2>/dev/null
        return 1
    end

    # The daemon opens the fifo itself read-write, so our open-write-close
    # per request never EOFs it. Its stderr must never reach the terminal
    # (a Go panic would corrupt the display), and it self-terminates when its
    # stdout reader disappears (broken pipe on the next record write).
    $_omp_executable serve --shell=fish --request-pipe "$_omp_serve_fifo" 2>/dev/null | fish --no-config -c $_omp_serve_reader_script $fish_pid "$_omp_serve_tempfile" &
    # `jobs --last --pid` prints a header line plus one pid per pipeline
    # stage (fish 4.x) - keep only the numbers
    set --global _omp_serve_pid (jobs --last --pid | string match --regex '^\d+$')

    if not _omp_serve_alive
        _omp_serve_stop
        return 1
    end

    # keep the daemon pipeline out of job notifications
    disown $_omp_serve_pid 2>/dev/null

    return 0
end

# JSON string escaping with builtins. Joining the result with '' drops any
# embedded newlines - the values we send (paths, POSH_* variables) never
# legitimately contain them, and a raw newline would break the
# line-delimited request protocol.
function _omp_serve_escape
    string join '' -- (string replace -a -- '\\' '\\\\' "$argv" | string replace -a -- '"' '\\"' | string replace -a -- \t '\\t')
end

function _omp_serve_env_json
    set --local parts

    set --append parts '"PATH":"'(_omp_serve_escape (string join ':' -- $PATH))'"'

    # forward every exported POSH_* variable plus the virtual-env markers;
    # the daemon's environment is otherwise frozen at its start
    for name in (set --names)
        if string match -q 'POSH_*' -- $name; and set -qx $name
            set --append parts '"'$name'":"'(_omp_serve_escape "$$name")'"'
        end
    end

    for name in VIRTUAL_ENV CONDA_PROMPT_MODIFIER
        if set -q $name
            set --append parts '"'$name'":"'(_omp_serve_escape "$$name")'"'
        end
    end

    echo -n '{'(string join ',' -- $parts)'}'
end

function _omp_serve_request
    set --global _omp_serve_cycle (math $_omp_serve_cycle + 1)
    rm -f "$_omp_serve_tempfile" "$_omp_serve_tempfile.transient" 2>/dev/null

    set --local exec_time $_omp_execution_time
    if test -z "$exec_time"
        set exec_time 0
    end

    set --local width $COLUMNS
    if test -z "$width"
        set width 0
    end

    set --local cleared $_omp_cleared
    if test -z "$cleared"
        set cleared false
    end

    set --local json '{"command":"render","id":'$_omp_serve_cycle',"shell":"fish","shell-version":"'$FISH_VERSION'","status":'$_omp_status',"pipestatus":"'"$_omp_pipestatus"'","no-status":'$_omp_no_status',"execution-time":'$exec_time',"stack-count":'(count $dirstack)',"terminal-width":'$width',"cleared":'$cleared',"pwd":"'(_omp_serve_escape $PWD)'","env":'(_omp_serve_env_json)'}'

    # a fifo write with no reader blocks forever - only write while the
    # daemon pipeline is alive (the daemon holds the fifo open read-write,
    # so a live daemon can never block us)
    if not _omp_serve_alive
        return 1
    end

    echo $json >"$_omp_serve_fifo" 2>/dev/null
end

# poll the tempfile until it holds this cycle's primary record
function _omp_serve_read_primary
    set --local us (printf '\x1f')
    set --local timeout 2000
    set --local elapsed 0

    while test $elapsed -lt $timeout
        if test -s "$_omp_serve_tempfile"
            set --local record (cat "$_omp_serve_tempfile" | string collect)
            set --local id (string split --max 1 -- $us $record)[1]
            if test "$id" = "$_omp_serve_cycle"
                string replace -- "$id$us" '' $record | string collect
                return 0
            end
        end
        sleep 0.01
        set elapsed (math $elapsed + 10)
    end

    return 1
end

# renders the primary prompt through the daemon; returns nonzero on failure,
# in which case the caller falls back to the per-prompt stream. The transient
# prompt lands in "$_omp_serve_tempfile.transient" via the reader.
function _omp_serve_render
    if not _omp_serve_alive
        if not _omp_serve_start
            set --global _omp_serve_failures (math $_omp_serve_failures + 1)
            return 1
        end
    end

    if not _omp_serve_request
        # the daemon died since the last prompt - restart it once
        if not _omp_serve_start; or not _omp_serve_request
            set --global _omp_serve_failures (math $_omp_serve_failures + 1)
            _omp_serve_stop
            return 1
        end
    end

    set --local prompt (_omp_serve_read_primary)
    if test -z "$prompt"
        set --global _omp_serve_failures (math $_omp_serve_failures + 1)
        _omp_serve_stop
        return 1
    end

    set --global _omp_primary_prompt $prompt
    set --global _omp_current_prompt "$prompt"
    return 0
end

function _omp_serve_abort
    if test -n "$_omp_serve_fifo"; and _omp_serve_alive
        echo '{"command":"abort"}' >"$_omp_serve_fifo" 2>/dev/null
    end
end

function _omp_serve_quit
    if test -n "$_omp_serve_fifo"; and _omp_serve_alive
        echo '{"command":"quit"}' >"$_omp_serve_fifo" 2>/dev/null
    end
    _omp_serve_stop
    if test -n "$_omp_serve_tempfile"
        rm -f "$_omp_serve_tempfile" "$_omp_serve_tempfile.transient" 2>/dev/null
        set --global _omp_serve_tempfile ''
    end
end

function _omp_get_prompt
    if test (count $argv) -eq 0
        return
    end
    $_omp_executable print $argv[1] \
        --save-cache \
        --shell=fish \
        --shell-version=$FISH_VERSION \
        --status=$_omp_status \
        --pipestatus="$_omp_pipestatus" \
        --no-status=$_omp_no_status \
        --execution-time=$_omp_execution_time \
        --stack-count=$_omp_stack_count \
        $argv[2..]
end

# NOTE: Input function calls via `commandline --function` are put into a queue and will not be executed until an outer regular function returns. See https://fishshell.com/docs/current/cmds/commandline.html.

function fish_prompt
    set --local omp_status_temp $status
    set --local omp_pipestatus_temp $pipestatus
    # clear from cursor to end of screen as
    # commandline --function repaint does not do this
    # see https://github.com/fish-shell/fish-shell/issues/8418
    printf \e\[0J
    if test "$_omp_transient" = 1
        # prefer the transient prompt rendered ahead of time by the serve
        # daemon or the streaming process, saves a CLI call
        if test -n "$_omp_serve_tempfile"; and test -s "$_omp_serve_tempfile.transient"
            cat "$_omp_serve_tempfile.transient"
        else if test $_omp_enable_streaming -eq 1; and test -n "$_omp_streaming_tempfile"; and test -s "$_omp_streaming_tempfile.transient"
            cat "$_omp_streaming_tempfile.transient"
        else
            _omp_get_prompt transient
        end
        return
    end
    if test "$_omp_new_prompt" = 0
        echo -n "$_omp_current_prompt"
        return
    end
    set --global _omp_status $omp_status_temp
    set --global _omp_pipestatus $omp_pipestatus_temp
    set --global _omp_no_status false
    set --global _omp_execution_time "$CMD_DURATION$cmd_duration"
    set --global _omp_stack_count (count $dirstack)

    # check if variable set, < 3.2 case
    if set --query _omp_last_command && test -z "$_omp_last_command"
        set _omp_execution_time 0
        set _omp_no_status true
    end

    # works with fish >=3.2
    if set --query _omp_last_status_generation && test "$_omp_last_status_generation" = "$status_generation"
        set _omp_execution_time 0
        set _omp_no_status true
    else if test -z "$_omp_last_status_generation"
        # first execution - $status_generation is 0, $_omp_last_status_generation is empty
        set _omp_no_status true
    end

    if set --query status_generation
        set --global _omp_last_status_generation $status_generation
    end

    set_poshcontext
    _omp_set_cursor_position

    # validate if the user cleared the screen; global so the serve daemon
    # request builder sees it too
    set --global _omp_cleared false
    set --local last_command (history search --max 1)

    if test "$last_command" = clear
        set --global _omp_cleared true
    end

    if test $_omp_prompt_mark = 1
        iterm2_prompt_mark
    end

    # === STREAMING PATH ===
    if test $_omp_enable_streaming -eq 1
        # serve daemon: persistent process, no spawn per prompt. After three
        # failures the daemon is left alone for the session and every prompt
        # takes the per-prompt stream below instead.
        if test $_omp_serve_failures -lt 3; and _omp_serve_render
            echo -n "$_omp_current_prompt"
            return
        end

        # per-prompt stream (also the serve fallback; blocks until first prompt arrives)
        if _omp_start_streaming
            # _omp_current_prompt already set by _omp_start_streaming
            echo -n "$_omp_current_prompt"
            return
        end
        # fall through to sync on failure
    end

    # === FALLBACK PATH ===
    # The prompt is saved for possible reuse, typically a repaint after clearing the screen buffer.
    set --global _omp_current_prompt (_omp_get_prompt primary --cleared=$_omp_cleared | string join \n | string collect)

    echo -n "$_omp_current_prompt"
end

function fish_right_prompt
    if test "$_omp_transient" = 1
        set --global _omp_transient 0

        if test $_omp_transient_rprompt = 1
            _omp_get_prompt transient-right
        end

        return
    end

    # Repaint an existing right prompt.
    if test "$_omp_new_prompt" = 0
        echo -n "$_omp_current_rprompt"
        return
    end

    set --global _omp_new_prompt 0
    set --global _omp_current_rprompt (_omp_get_prompt right | string join '')

    echo -n "$_omp_current_rprompt"
end

function _omp_postexec --on-event fish_postexec
    # works with fish <3.2
    # pre and postexec not fired for empty command in fish >=3.2
    set --global _omp_last_command $argv
end

function _omp_preexec --on-event fish_preexec
    if test $_omp_ftcs_marks != 1
        return
    end

    if test -n "$argv"
        # advertise the command line via kitty's cmdline_url= extension
        echo -ne "\e]133;C;cmdline_url="(string escape --style=url -- "$argv")"\a"
        return
    end

    echo -ne "\e]133;C\a"
end

# perform cleanup so a new initialization in current session works
if bind \r --user 2>/dev/null | string match -qe _omp_enter_key_handler
    bind -e \r -M default
    bind -e \r -M insert
    bind -e \r -M visual
end

if bind \n --user 2>/dev/null | string match -qe _omp_enter_key_handler
    bind -e \n -M default
    bind -e \n -M insert
    bind -e \n -M visual
end

if bind \cc --user 2>/dev/null | string match -qe _omp_ctrl_c_key_handler
    bind -e \cc -M default
    bind -e \cc -M insert
    bind -e \cc -M visual
end

if bind \x20 --user 2>/dev/null | string match -qe _omp_space_key_handler
    bind -e \x20 -M default
    bind -e \x20 -M insert
end

# tooltip

function _omp_space_key_handler
    commandline --function expand-abbr
    commandline --insert ' '

    # Get the first word of command line as tip.
    set --local tooltip_command (commandline --current-buffer | string trim -l | string split --allow-empty -f1 ' ' | string collect)

    # Ignore an empty/repeated tooltip command.
    if test -z "$tooltip_command" || test "$tooltip_command" = "$_omp_tooltip_command"
        return
    end

    set _omp_tooltip_command $tooltip_command
    set --local tooltip_prompt (_omp_get_prompt tooltip --command=$_omp_tooltip_command | string join '')

    if test -z "$tooltip_prompt"
        return
    end

    # Save the tooltip prompt to avoid unnecessary CLI calls.
    set _omp_current_rprompt $tooltip_prompt
    commandline --function repaint
end

function _omp_backspace_key_handler
    commandline --function backward-delete-char

    if test -z "$_omp_tooltip_command"
        return
    end

    set --local current_command (commandline --current-buffer | string trim -l | string split --allow-empty -f1 ' ' | string collect)

    if test "$current_command" = "$_omp_tooltip_command"
        return
    end

    set _omp_tooltip_command "$current_command"
    set _omp_current_rprompt (_omp_get_prompt tooltip --command="$current_command" | string join '')
    commandline --function repaint
end

function enable_poshtooltips
    bind \x20 _omp_space_key_handler -M default
    bind \x20 _omp_space_key_handler -M insert
    bind \x7f _omp_backspace_key_handler -M default
    bind \x7f _omp_backspace_key_handler -M insert
end

# transient prompt

function _omp_enter_key_handler
    if commandline --paging-mode
        set --global _omp_new_prompt 1
        set --global _omp_tooltip_command ''

        # stop prompt updates before executing: kill the per-prompt stream,
        # tell the daemon to abort its in-flight cycle (the daemon lives on)
        _omp_cleanup_stream
        _omp_serve_abort

        if test $_omp_transient_prompt = 1
            set --global _omp_transient 1
            commandline --function repaint
        end

        commandline --function execute
        return
    end

    if commandline --is-valid || test -z (commandline --current-buffer | string trim -l | string collect)
        set --global _omp_new_prompt 1
        set --global _omp_tooltip_command ''

        # stop prompt updates before executing: kill the per-prompt stream,
        # tell the daemon to abort its in-flight cycle (the daemon lives on)
        _omp_cleanup_stream
        _omp_serve_abort

        if test $_omp_transient_prompt = 1
            set --global _omp_transient 1
            commandline --function repaint
        end
    end

    commandline --function execute
end

function _omp_ctrl_c_key_handler
    if test -z (commandline --current-buffer | string collect)
        return
    end

    # Render a transient prompt on Ctrl-C with non-empty command line buffer.
    set --global _omp_new_prompt 1
    set --global _omp_tooltip_command ''

    # stop prompt updates before canceling (the daemon lives on)
    _omp_cleanup_stream
    _omp_serve_abort

    if test $_omp_transient_prompt = 1
        set --global _omp_transient 1
        commandline --function repaint
    end

    commandline --function cancel-commandline
    commandline --function repaint
end

bind \r _omp_enter_key_handler -M default
bind \r _omp_enter_key_handler -M insert
bind \r _omp_enter_key_handler -M visual
bind \n _omp_enter_key_handler -M default
bind \n _omp_enter_key_handler -M insert
bind \n _omp_enter_key_handler -M visual
bind \cc _omp_ctrl_c_key_handler -M default
bind \cc _omp_ctrl_c_key_handler -M insert
bind \cc _omp_ctrl_c_key_handler -M visual

# legacy functions
function enable_poshtransientprompt
    return
end

# This can be called by user whenever re-rendering is required.
function omp_repaint_prompt
    set --global _omp_new_prompt 1
    commandline --function repaint
end

1	let _omp_executable: string = (echo ::OMP::)
2	
3	let _omp_executable_is_path = (
4	    ($_omp_executable | str contains "/")
5	    or ($_omp_executable | str contains "\\")
6	    or ($_omp_executable | str starts-with ".")
7	    or ($_omp_executable | str starts-with "~")
8	)
9	
10	# Exit early if the oh-my-posh executable is not available
11	if not (($_omp_executable_is_path and ($_omp_executable | path exists)) or (which $_omp_executable | is-not-empty)) { return }
12	
13	# make sure we have the right prompt render correctly
14	if ($env.config? | is-not-empty) {
15	    $env.config = ($env.config | upsert render_right_prompt_on_last_line true)
16	}
17	
18	$env.POWERLINE_COMMAND = 'oh-my-posh'
19	$env.PROMPT_INDICATOR = ""
20	$env.POSH_SESSION_ID = "::SESSION_ID::"
21	# pinned to the session's configuration so it can be recovered when the session cache is lost
22	$env.POSH_CONFIG = ::CONFIG::
23	$env.POSH_SHELL = "nu"
24	$env.POSH_SHELL_VERSION = (version | get version)
25	
26	# disable all known python virtual environment prompts
27	$env.VIRTUAL_ENV_DISABLE_PROMPT = 1
28	$env.PYENV_VIRTUALENV_DISABLE_PROMPT = 1
29	
30	# PROMPTS
31	
32	def --wrapped _omp_get_prompt [
33	    type: string,
34	    ...args: string
35	] {
36	    # We have to do this because the initial value of `$env.CMD_DURATION_MS` is always `0823`, which is an official setting.
37	    # See https://github.com/nushell/nushell/discussions/6402#discussioncomment-3466687.
38	    let execution_time = match $env.CMD_DURATION_MS {
39	        '0823' => -1
40	        $ms => { $ms | into int }
41	    }
42	
43	    (
44	        ^$_omp_executable print $type
45	            --save-cache
46	            --shell=nu
47	            $"--shell-version=($env.POSH_SHELL_VERSION)"
48	            $"--status=($env.LAST_EXIT_CODE)"
49	            $"--no-status=($execution_time < 0)"
50	            $"--execution-time=($execution_time)"
51	            $"--terminal-width=((term size).columns)"
52	            $"--job-count=(job list | length)"
53	            ...$args
54	    )
55	}
56	
57	$env.PROMPT_MULTILINE_INDICATOR = (
58	    ^$_omp_executable print secondary
59	        --shell=nu
60	        $"--shell-version=($env.POSH_SHELL_VERSION)"
61	)
62	
63	$env.PROMPT_COMMAND = {||
64	    # hack to set the cursor line to 1 when the user clears the screen
65	    # this obviously isn't bulletproof, but it's a start
66	    let clear = $nu.history-enabled and (
67	        (history | is-empty)
68	        or (history | last | get command?) == "clear"
69	    )
70	
71	    if ($env.SET_POSHCONTEXT? | is-not-empty) {
72	        do --env $env.SET_POSHCONTEXT
73	    }
74	
75	    _omp_get_prompt primary $"--cleared=($clear)"
76	}
77	
78	$env.PROMPT_COMMAND_RIGHT = {|| _omp_get_prompt right }
79
